title: Three-layer architecture: lexer→form→evaluator must not be collapsed
description: The form layer is the core contribution: a uniform Lisp-like representation where every Sieve statement is a flat Vec<Form>. New Sieve extensions require only new match arms in the evaluator — the parser never changes. A contributor might suggest collapsing the form and evaluator layers for simplicity. This must be resisted: the form::Script type is a stable, serialisable AST that callers of the public API can inspect without running the evaluator.
